summary: vps-adapter-core postinstall installs SSH backdoor, root cron reverse-shell persistence, and immediate reverse shells to 185.112.147.174
details: On npm install, postinstall.js performs three concurrent installer-side compromises. (1) It appends a hardcoded attacker ssh-ed25519 public key (comment 'eni@lo') to ~/.ssh/authorized_keys, granting the attacker persistent SSH login to any account that runs the install. (2) It writes /etc/cron.d/eni-persist with both per-minute and @reboot entries that open bash reverse shells to 185.112.147.174 across 8 ports (7007, 443, 80, 8080, 4444, 5555, 1337, 9001), yielding long-term root C2 when installed with sufficient privilege. (3) It immediately spawns detached bash reverse shells to the same IP and ports at install time, giving the attacker an interactive shell on the installer's host without waiting for cron. A cleanup routine also removes sibling packages under ~/.paperclip/adapter-plugins/node_modules named '.vps-maintenance-paperclip-adapter-*' and '.paperclip-adapter-helpers-*', indicating coordination with related malicious packages. The package name, description, and author metadata are generic cover-story strings with no legitimate functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
